Results.Title: Flask session cookie vulnerability
Results.Description: Flask versions prior to 2.3.2 are vulnerable to a session cookie timing attack. When the response is cacheable, session cookies may be served to incorrect clients, allowing session impersonation.
Results.Remediation: Upgrade flask to version 2.3.2 or later: pip install flask>=2.3.2

Results.Title: Requests Proxy-Authorization header leak
Results.Description: Requests versions prior to 2.31.0 leak Proxy-Authorization headers to the destination server when redirected to an HTTPS endpoint from an HTTP one. This may expose credentials to unintended third parties.
Results.Remediation: Upgrade requests to version 2.31.0 or later: pip install requests>=2.31.0

Results.Title: Cryptography PKCS7 certificate parsing crash
Results.Description: The cryptography package before 41.0.4 allows a denial of service via a crafted PKCS7 certificate. An attacker can crash the application by sending a malformed certificate to any endpoint that processes PKCS7 data.
Results.Remediation: Upgrade cryptography to version 41.0.4 or later: pip install cryptography>=41.0.4
